title: Target Enrichment via In-Solution Hybridisation Using Twist Bioscience Panels
author: Tina Saupe

steps.instruction: A list of required reagents , their the preparation, and equipment can be found in Materials.

(A) Set the Thermo mixer using the 1.5 ml Smartblock at 65 °C at the start of the day. (B) Fetch ice, to keep reagents on ice. (C) Retrieve dsDNA libraries from the freezer and allow them to thaw at Room temperature Spin down and homogenise by pipetting before transferring to the respective 0.2 mL, PCR tube(s) . (Refer to section 3)

Thermo cycler setting: Two Thermal cycler are needed for Day 1! (A) Program the first Thermal cycler and make sure that the lid is heated to at least 105 °C . NB! For the preparation of the solution master mixes. A B C 1 temperature time 2 activation 95°C ∞ (B) Program the second Thermal cycler and make sure the lid is heated to at least 65 °C . NB! For the incubation of the hybridisation reactions. A B C 1 temperature time 2 activation 62°C ∞

Please read Materials to prepare the reagents prior to the start of the procedure. Preparation for the target enrichment using the 'alternate pre-hybridisation DNA concentration' protocol from Twist Bioscience.
(A) Set two Thermo mixer, using a 1.5 mL Smartblock and a 50 mL respectively, at 49 °C at the start of the day. (B) Fetch Ice prior to preparation of the Post-capture PCR amplification.

(A) Place the Dynabeads™ Streptavidin for Target Enrichment Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #65606D on a rotator for at least 00:20:00 . (B) Make 1X B&W buffer from the prepared 2X B&W buffer in a 1:1 dilution . (C) Aliquot the total respective amounts for 1X and 2X B&W buffers described in the Note. Per hybridisation reaction you need in total 600 µL, 2X B&W buffer at Room temperature 2500 µL, 1X B&W buffer 1 at Room temperature 2100 µL, 1X B&W buffer 2 heated at 49 °C in a thermal mixer.
Prepare the dsDNA libraries for the hybridisation reaction.
Transfer the repective volume or 10 µL of each dsDNA library to new 0.2 mL, PCR tube(s) .

(A) Aliquot the total amount of Hybridization Enhancer Twist Bioscience Catalog #100937, 100986, 101266 for each reaction into a 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . Store at Room temperature . (B) Prepare the Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 . Vortex pre-equilibrated Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 until homogenised mixed. NB! Vortex until all Beads are removed from the outer surface. Aliquot the total amount of Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 into a 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . (D) Prepare fresh 80 % volume, Ethanol . (E) Heat the Hybridization Mix Twist Bioscience Catalog #100212, 100587, 100528, 104235 at 65 °C in the Thermo mixer for 00:10:00 , or until all precipitate is dissolved, then cool to Room temperature on the Benchtop for at least 00:05:00 . The volume of the Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 is 1.8x more than the input of the library. volume [µl] = input * 1.8
Add 1.8x of Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 to each dsDNA library in the 0.2 mL, PCR tube(s) . Mix by briefly pulse-spinning. Quick spin-down.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Washing Steps. Perform this step for a total of 3 times. NB! Keep the tube(s) on the magnetic rack.
Wash the bead pellet by gently adding 250 µL of 80 % volume, Ethanol . NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Incubate for 00:01:00 .
Remove and discard the ethanol.
Incubate the tube(s) containing the Probes solution MM at 95 °C for 00:05:00 in the Thermo cycler .
Add 0.1 % volume of TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 to each B&W buffer.
After the last wash, use a 10 µL, Pipette to remove all traces of supernatant. NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Prepare Blockers solution MM in a 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . A B C 1 reagents per reaction [µl] MM (n*reagent) 2 Blockers solution 5.0 3 Universal Blockers 7.0 4 Total 12.0

substeps.instruction: Prepare Blockers solution MM in a
mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . A B C 1 reagents per reaction [µl] MM (n*reagent) 2 Blockers solution

steps.instruction: Prepare Probes solution MM in a 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . A B C 1 reagents per reaction [µl] MM (n*reagent) 2 Hybridisation Mix 5.0 3 Twist Custom panel 1.0 4 Twist secondary panel 0.5 5 nuclease-free molecular biology-grade water 3.5 6 Total 10.0

substeps.instruction: Prepare Probes solution MM in a
mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . A B C 1 reagents per reaction [µl] MM (n*reagent) 2 Hybridisation Mix

steps.instruction: Place the 0.2 mL, PCR tube(s) on a magnetic rack for 00:05:00 . NB! Wait until all beads from a pellet. The tube(s) should contain a clear solution.
Do not remove the tube(s) from the magnet rack! Remove and discard the supernatant.
Air-dry the pellet on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 or until the pellet are dry. Prepare the Blockers solution master mix while waiting for the air-dry. (Refer to section 12).
Remove the tube(s) from the magnetic rack.

Add 12 µL, Blockers solution Master Mix to the dried dsDNA libraries and homogenise by pipetting. Tip! Wash the pellet carefully down with the Blockers solution mix without pulling beads into the tip.
Resuspend and briefly spin down the Blockers solution MM. Store on ice until the transfer to the dried dsDNA libraries (Refer to step 11).
Resuspend and brielfy centrifuge the Probe solution MM .
For each hybridisation reaction, aliquot 10 µL, Probe solution MM to 0.2 µL, PCR tube(s) . Briefly spin down and store on ice.
Incubate the Blockers solution reaction tube(s) containing the eluted dsDNA libraries at 95 °C for 00:05:00 .
After the incubation, remove the tube(s) from the Thermo cycler and place immediately cool on ice for 00:05:00 .
Place the Probes and Blockers solution reaction tube(s) on the bench at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Carefully mix the Probes solution reactions by flicking the tube(s). Briefly spin down.

Pipette 10 µL of the Probes solution reactions to each Blockers solution reaction tube(s). Homogenise by pipetting up and down for 4-5 times. Briefly spin down. Ensure all solution is at the bottom of the tube(s).
Add 30 µL of Hybridisation Enhancer to the top of the hybridisation reaction. Homogenise by pipetting up and down for 4-5 times. Briefly spin down. Ensure that there are no bubbles present.
Place the tube(s) in the thermal cycler at 65 °C for 16:00:00 .

Aliquot 300 µL, Streptavidin Binding Beads for each reaction into a 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . NB! Pipette Streptavidin Binding Beads carefully to ensure all beads are in the tube(s). NB! Change the tip every time.
Place the tube(s) on a magnetic rack for 00:02:00 . NB! Wait until all beads from a pellet. The tube(s) should contain a clear solution.
Open the thermal cycler lid. Quick spin-down.

Transfer each hybridisation reaction (~52 µl) to one 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) containing the cleaned Streptavidin Binding Beads. NB! The Hybridisation enhancer may interfer with pipette everything into the binding bead tube(s). Try to pipette carefully and check if nothing in the tip is left.
Heat the 1x B&W buffer 2 at 49 °C in a thermal mixer.
Remove and discard the supernatant. NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Resuspend the bead pellet using 600 µL, 1X B&W buffer 1 .
Washing Steps. Perform this step for a total of 3 times.
Remove the tube(s) from the magnet rack.
Remove and discard the supernatant. NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Add 600 µL, 2X B&W buffer and resuspend the beads by vortexing until homogenised. Briefly centrifuge to remove bubbles from the lid.
Remove the tube(s) from the magnet rack!
Place the tube(s) in the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 . NB! Wait until all beads from a pellet. The tube(s) should contain a clear solution.
Mix by pipetting up and down. NB! This step is critical for minimising the off-target binding. Do not let the hybridisation reaction cool down!
Incubate the tube(s) containing the hybridisation reactions and Streptavidin Binding Beads on a table rotator for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .
Remove the 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) from the table rotator and quick spin-down to ensure all solution is at the bottom of the tube(s).

Program the Thermal cycler and set the lid temperature to 105 °C . A B C D 1 temperature time cycles 2 initialisation 98 °C 45 sec 3 denaturation 98 °C 15 sec 15 4 annealing 60 °C 30 sec 5 extension 72 °C 30 sec 6 final extension 72 °C 1 min 7 hold 4 °C ∞
Remove and store the hybridisation reaction in a new 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) ~ It contains the non-enriched library. Freeze the non-enriched library at -20 °C .
Place the 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) on the magnetic rack for 00:02:00 .
Move the entire volume of each hybridisation reaction (~700 µl) into a new 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) .
Quick spin-down.
Remove the 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) from the magnetic rack and add 700 µL, 1X B&W buffer 1 . Resuspend by pipetting carefully up and down or flicking carefully.
Remove the tube(s) from the magnetic rack.
Remove and discard the clear supernatant. NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Place the tube(s) on the magnetic rack for 00:01:00 . NB! Wait until all beads from a pellet. The tube(s) should contain a clear solution.
Remove and discard the clear supernatant. NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Washing Steps. Perform this step for a total of 3 times. Keep the 1X B&W buffer 2 heated at 49 °C all the time.
Add 700 µL, 1B B&W buffer 2 to each tube. Briefly resuspend by pipetting 2-3 times. Centrifuge briefly.
Incubate the tube(s) for 00:05:00 at 49 °C .
Place the tube(s) on the magnetic rack for 00:01:00 .
Remove the tube(s) from the magnetic rack .
After the last wash, use a 10 µL, Pipette to remove all traces of supernatant. NB! Do not disturb the pellet.

Remove the tube(s) from the magnetic rack and add 45 µL, nuclease-free molecular biology-grade water or EB buffer Qiagen Catalog #19086 (for long storage) to each hybridisation reaction. Resuspend briefly by pipetting carefully up and down. Incubate on ice.
For each hybridisation reaction, aliquot 27.5 µL, Ammplification MM to a 0.2 µL, PCR tube(s) .
Prepare Amplification MM in a 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . A B C 1 reagents pre reaction [µl] MM (n*1.05*reagent) 2 Equinox Library Amp Mix (2x) 25.0 3 Amplification Primer 2.5
Add 22.5 µL, hybridisation reaction to the 0.2 µL, PCR tube(s) . Quick spin-down.
Place the tube(s) in the thermal cycler, close the lid and start the cycler program.

(A) Prepare the Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 . Vortex pre-equilibrated Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 until homogenised mixed. NB! Vortex until all Beads are removed from the outer surface. Aliquot the total amount of Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 into a 1.5 mL, LoBind Eppendorf tube(s) . (B) Prepare fresh 80 % volume, Ethanol .
Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Add 90 µL of Ampure XP beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63881 to eac 0.2 mL, PCR tube(s) . Mix by vortexing. Quick spin-down.
Place the 0.2 mL, PCR tube(s) on a magnetic rack for 00:05:00 . NB! Wait until all beads from a pellet. The tube(s) should contain a clear solution.
Do not remove the tube(s) from the magnet rack! Remove and discard the supernatant.
Washing Steps. Perform this step for a total of 3 times. NB! Keep the tube(s) on the magnetic rack.
Wash the bead pellet by gently adding 250 µL of 80 % volume, Ethanol . NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Incubate for 00:01:00 .
Remove and discard the ethanol.
After the last wash, use a 10 µL, Pipette to remove all traces of supernatant. NB! Do not disturb the pellet.
Air-dry the pellet on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 or until the pellet are dry.
Remove the tube(s) from the magnetic rack.
Add 12 µL, EB buffer to the dried hybridisation reactions and homogenise by pipetting. Tip! Wash the pellet carefully down with the EB buffer without pulling beads into the tip.
Use 2 µL, enriched dsDNA library to measure the DNA concentrations using Qubit and Fragment analyser. Read instrument manuals for further instructions.
